page: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server utilizes a shared CPU allocation model. It includes 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address, with no dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate moderate single-core capabilities alongside limitations in multi-core and memory throughput. The stress-ng single-core CPU test yields an average score of 1455.08, while the multi-core score drops to a weak 2890.77, reflecting the shared CPU resource model. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with cached small-block reads reaching 206.26 GB/sec, and uncached large-block reads and writes falling into the bottom 10% tier at 16.98 GB/sec and 14.27 GB/sec respectively. Single-threaded gzip compression performs at 23.52 GB/sec, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves an average rate of 9.07 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU architecture and modest memory footprint position this server as a budget-friendly option for workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated compute resources. The inclusion of 100 GB NVMe SSD storage provides fast local I/O relative to the resource class. This configuration is best suited for lightweight applications, development environments, basic web serving, and small-scale utility scripts.
description: Vultr vhp-2c-4gb-intel is a virtual server from the High Performance family, configured with 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor running at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks a dedicated GPU and provides a single public IPv4 address. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1455.08, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the lower percentiles due to the shared CPU allocation. This resource profile offers a budget-friendly entry point for lightweight workloads, making it suitable for development environments, basic web hosting, and low-intensity background tasks.
